faqs.question: Are yellow jackets dangerous to my Carteret family or pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are highly aggressive and can sting multiple times, defending their nests vigorously when threatened. They pose serious risks to children, pets, and anyone with allergies. Unlike honeybees, yellow jackets don't lose their stinger and can attack repeatedly, making professional removal essential for safety.

faqs.question: When is yellow jacket season in Carteret, NJ?
faqs.answer: Yellow jacket activity in Carteret typically peaks from late summer through early fall (August to October) when colonies reach maximum size. However, nests begin forming in spring, so early detection and removal prevent colonies from growing to dangerous sizes. We provide yellow jacket services year-round as New Jersey's climate can support activity into November.
